meta.title: Project Inquiry Checklist | WEI LAN Resources
meta.description: Prepare waste type, capacity, site footprint, output requirements, photos, videos, and project stage before requesting a WEI LAN recycling equipment proposal.

bodyHtml:
Checklist

Project Inquiry Checklist

A practical RFQ preparation guide for sending enough project information to receive a more relevant equipment recommendation.

Type: RFQ checklist Updated: 2026-06-30 Buyer fit: Proposal preparation Review time: 7 min

Free preview

Prepare a clearer first project brief

This checklist helps your team collect the operating inputs WEI LAN needs for a useful first engineering review. Start with what is already known and mark uncertain items for follow-up.

01

Waste type and material composition

Identify the main material stream, typical composition, contamination, moisture, particle or object size, and any materials that must be removed or recovered.

For plastics, note PET, HDPE, PP, film, color groups, and mixed recyclable fractions. For construction or bulky waste, note aggregate, wood, metal, plastic, textile, and light-material content.

02

Target capacity and operating schedule

Share the target hourly throughput, operating hours per day, shifts per day, expected peak periods, and any planned future expansion.

Separating current demand from future capacity helps the first recommendation avoid both undersizing and unnecessary equipment.

Resource access

Unlock all resources

Submit your business contact details once to read the complete checklist and unlock the WEI LAN resource library in this browser.

By submitting, you agree that WEI LAN may contact you about this resource and related project support.

Access confirmed

All resources unlocked

You can now read the complete checklist. Other gated WEI LAN resources will open without another form in this browser.

Full checklist

03

Site footprint and installation conditions

Provide the project country, available plant or yard dimensions, clear height, access limits, voltage and frequency, and any restrictions affecting transport, lifting, installation, dust, or noise control.

04

Expected output products and quality targets

List the fractions your team wants to recover, the required grouping or purity direction, downstream use, packaging method, and any local acceptance requirements.

05

Material photos, videos, and current process

Prepare representative input photos or videos, current process diagrams when available, and examples of existing manual or mechanical sorting. Show normal material as well as difficult or contaminated batches.

06

Project stage, timeline, and required documents

State whether the project is in research, budget approval, tender, supplier comparison, replacement, or quotation stage. Include the target decision timeline and documents needed for the next internal review.

Next step

Turn the checklist into an engineering inquiry

Combine the information above into one project brief, then send it through the contact form for configuration review and proposal discussion.

Send Project Details

Prepare a stronger RFQ

Pair the checklist with product brochures and technical sheets before contacting engineering.
